data.title: OpenAI Announces Data Center Energy Cost Control Plan
data.description: OpenAI announced its "Stargate Community plan" on Tuesday, aiming to achieve energy self-sufficiency and ensure its operations do not increase electricity costs for local communities. The Stargate project, a multi-year, $500 billion initiative to build AI data centers for training and inference, has garnered support from major investors including Oracle. As energy access becomes a key constraint on AI development, many tech companies are directly investing in power infrastructure to support larger and more numerous data centers. OpenAI stated that each Stargate site will have a tailored community plan, "driven by community input and local needs." "Specifically, this could include providing entirely new dedicated power and storage facilities for the project (fully funded by the project team), or increasing and paying for new energy generation and transmission resources." (Jinshi)

data.title: Opinion: The World Liberty Fi team manipulated governance voting to sacrifice holders' interests and cash out.
data.description: DeFi^2 published an article on the X platform stating that the governance vote of World Liberty Fi (WLFI) this month was manipulated. Bubble Maps shows that most of the major voters were team or strategic partner wallets. The vote forcibly passed a $1 growth plan to sell WLFI tokens, while tokens held by genuine investors have remained locked since TGE and cannot participate in the unlocking vote. According to the project's official documentation, 75% of the protocol revenue went to the Trump family and 25% to the Witkoff family, with WLFI holders having no right to any protocol revenue. The team has already transferred 500 million WLFI tokens to Jump Trading. DeFi^2 believes that due to WLFI's lack of governance rights and revenue sharing, and the pressure from the foundation to sell off its holdings, its $17 billion valuation lacks intrinsic value support. DeFi^2 has been intermittently shorting WLFI since the pre-market price was above $0.34 and expects the token price to continue to fall due to dilution and deliberate cashing out.

data.title: CryptoQuant CEO: Coinbase's privacy-protecting measures "disrupt" wallets, raising the bar for on-chain data analytics.
data.description: CryptoQuant CEO Ki Young Ju posted on the X platform, seemingly expressing dissatisfaction with Coinbase's wallet organization model. He stated that Coinbase frequently shuffles wallets in the name of privacy protection. Furthermore, Coinbase's published AOP (Account Balance) mixes exchange and custodian account balances, making it difficult to accurately distinguish between pure exchange reserves and custodian reserves, thus raising the barrier to on-chain data analysis. CryptoQuant reverse-engineered these recent changes and adjusted its custom clustering logic to track and capture most real-time Bitcoin inflows/outflows. Currently, their platform only labels wallets 100% certain to belong to Coinbase (to avoid false positives). Some platforms may display higher reserve figures, but this does not necessarily indicate higher accuracy.

data.title: China's Technological Advancements Highlighted in Recent Briefing
data.description:
A recent press briefing by China's State Council Information Office outlined the country's progress in industrial and information technology development by 2025. According to Foresight News, the briefing highlighted that technologies such as perovskite materials, permanent magnet materials, and power batteries are at an internationally advanced level. Additionally, advancements in new networks, high-speed communication, advanced computing, emerging software, and blockchain technologies are accelerating.

The development of prototypes based on new quantum technologies, such as atomic clocks and magnetometers, has been successful. Furthermore, superconducting quantum computers and optical quantum computers have achieved quantum superiority, possessing the capability to solve specific problems beyond the reach of classical computers. The application of brain-computer interface technology is expanding rapidly from the medical field to education and industry.

data.title: Survey Reveals Bitcoin Holders Rarely Spend Cryptocurrency
data.description:
A recent survey by Bitcoin mining company GoMining indicates that over 55% of cryptocurrency holders rarely or never spend Bitcoin. According to ChainCatcher, nearly 80% of respondents support broader adoption and believe in Bitcoin as a payment method.

The survey participants identified limited merchant acceptance, high fees, and privacy and security concerns as the main barriers to Bitcoin payments. GoMining CEO Mark Zalan noted that users desire a seamless experience when spending cryptocurrency and wish to receive rewards for their expenditures.

Despite these challenges, data from Artemis shows that monthly transactions via crypto cards have surged from $100 million at the beginning of 2023 to $1.5 billion by the end of 2025, indicating improvements in payment infrastructure.

data.title: North Korean-Linked Cyber Attacks Target Developers via Malicious VS Code Projects
data.description: 23pds posted on X. Reports have emerged that individuals associated with North Korea are using deceptive recruitment tactics to lure developers into opening malicious Visual Studio Code projects. These projects, once opened, automatically execute hidden tasks that retrieve JavaScript from Vercel and deploy backdoors, enabling remote code execution. Concerns have been raised as this attack method and its code were reportedly visible on GitHub in the repository 'VSCode-Backdoor' seven months ago, yet it has only recently gained widespread attention.

data.title: Potential Collapse of Crypto Market Structure Bill Raises Concerns
data.description:
Galaxy CEO Mike Novogratz expressed concerns on the X platform about the potential failure of the crypto market structure bill. According to ChainCatcher, the disagreement over stablecoin yields is a key factor, highlighting U.S. politics overshadowing effective policy-making. Banks are reportedly opposed to crypto platforms offering rewards to users, fearing the current situation if the bill is rejected. Novogratz suggests that if the market structure bill is disrupted, banks and their supporting Republican and Democratic senators will be responsible, with American consumers being the biggest losers. He hopes rational thinking will prevail.

Novogratz also noted that gold prices indicate the U.S. dollar is rapidly losing its status as a reserve currency, and the sell-off of long-term bonds is not a positive sign. Bitcoin's performance has been disappointing due to ongoing sell-offs, and its price needs to break through $100,000 to $103,000 to confirm a return to an upward trend.

data.title: Director of the Presidential Digital Assets Committee: We should seize the opportunity presented by a pro-crypto government to pass a market structure bill.
data.description: Patrick Witt, executive director of the President's Advisory Council on Digital Assets, stated in an article on the X platform that a cryptocurrency market structure bill is inevitable, and it's unrealistic to expect a multi-trillion-dollar industry to operate without a comprehensive regulatory framework. Witt pointed out that with a president who supports cryptocurrencies, control of Congress, and a regulatory environment provided by the SEC and CFTC, the industry should seize the opportunity to pass legislation rather than allowing Democrats to enact punitive legislation after a future financial crisis. Witt emphasized that while the CLARITY Act may not be perfect, compromises are necessary to secure 60 votes in the Senate, and perfection should not become an obstacle to the pursuit of excellence.

data.title: Galaxy CEO: Crypto Market Structure Bill May Fail; BTC Needs to Break $100,000-$103,000 to Reconfirm Uptrend
data.description: Galaxy CEO Mike Novogratz posted on the X platform that the Crypto Market Structure Act may fail due to the lack of agreement on stablecoin yields, a manifestation of American politics overriding sound policy. Banks do not want crypto platforms to offer rewards to users. If the bill is rejected, the current situation seems to be what banks fear. If this interferes with the market structure act, the blame will fall on the banks and the Republican and Democratic senators who support them, with American consumers being the biggest losers. Hopefully, calm thinking will prevail. Mike Novogratz added that gold prices indicate the dollar is rapidly losing its reserve currency status, the sell-off in long-term bonds is not a good sign, and Bitcoin's disappointing performance due to continued selling pressure requires a break above $100,000 to $103,000 to reconfirm a return to an upward trend.

data.title: Crypto markets fell across the board, with the CeFi sector leading the decline, dropping more than 5%, and ETH falling below $3,000.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market generally declined, with the CeFi sector leading the losses at 5.06% in the past 24 hours. Within the CeFi sector, OKB fell 4.99%, Aster (ASTER) fell 5.30%, and Binance Coin (BNB) fell 5.43%, but MX (MX) remained relatively resilient, rising 0.38%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 4.00%, briefly dipping below $88,000, and is currently trading at $89,000; Ethereum (ETH) fell 7.06%, breaking below $3,000. In other sectors, the Meme sector fell 3.09% in the last 24 hours, with SPX6900 (SPX) down 9.59%; the RWA sector fell 3.16%, but Keeta (KTA) rose 7.74%; the Layer 2 sector fell 4.12%, with SOON (SOON) down 8.10%; the PayFi sector fell 4.56%, with Monero (XMR) down 18.70%; the DeFi sector fell 4.60%, with River (RIVER) maintaining its upward trend, rising another 11.13% intraday; and the Layer 1 sector fell 4.63%, with Canton Network (CC) rising 10.62%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiLayer1, ssiCeFi, and ssiAI indices fell 6.21%, 5.38%, and 5.29%, respectively.

data.title: Shielded Labs receives $1.2 million in ZEC from the Winklevoss brothers to support the Zcash network.
data.description: Shielded Labs announced on the X platform that it has received a donation of 3,221 ZEC tokens, worth approximately $1.2 million, from Tyler Winklevoss and Cameron Winklevoss. This funding will support core initiatives such as network sustainability mechanisms, Crosslink, and dynamic fees, aiming to improve the long-term security, sustainability, and scalability of the Zcash network. This is the second donation from Tyler Winklevoss and Cameron Winklevoss to the organization; their first donation was made in 2023 to support the establishment of the Crosslink dedicated team. Shielded Labs is a Swiss-based Zcash support organization whose funding comes entirely from donations and operates independently of the developer fund and block rewards.
